g.text: The aggregation problem: percentiles do not add up
10 instances each report p99 = 200ms. Averaging gives 200ms - and is wrong. The math demands aggregating buckets first.
X The wrong way: average the pre-computed percentiles
avg(p99) = 200ms <- this is just the average of averages, not the global p99
Real global p99 is somewhere between 200ms and ... unknown. The slowest 1% may cluster on a few instances.
OK The correct way: sum the histogram buckets, then compute the quantile
